gmd:metadataStandardName.gco:CharacterString: ISO 19115-2 Geographic Information - Metadata Part 2 Extensions for imagery and gridded data

gmd:title.gco:CharacterString: 2010 USACE NCMP Topobathy Lidar: Atlantic Coast (NC, SC, GA, FL)

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: View the complete metadata record on InPort for more information about this dataset.

gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: NOAA's Office for Coastal Management (OCM) website

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: Information about the NOAA Office for Coastal Management (OCM)
Link to custom download, from the Data Access Viewer (DAV), the raster Digital Elevation Model (DEM) data that were created from this lidar data set.

gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: NOAA's Office for Coastal Management (OCM) Data Access Viewer (DAV)

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: The Data Access Viewer (DAV) allows a user to search for and download elevation, imagery, and land cover data for the coastal U.S. and its territories. The data, hosted by the NOAA Office for Coastal Management, can be customized and requested for free download through a checkout interface. An email provides a link to the customized data, while the original data set is available through a link within the viewer.

gmd:abstract.gco:CharacterString:
These files contain topographic and bathymetric lidar data collected as part of the National Coastal Mapping Program. Data acquisition occured between May and June 2010. The coverage area includes Florida, Georgia, South Carolina and North Carolina coasts from Cape Canaveral, FL to Cape Lookout, NC. Data coverage generally extends along the coastline from the waterline inland 500 meters.
This data was collected by a Leica ALS 50 Phase II Plus, Digital Aerial Camera System (DACS II) and CASI 1500 Hyperspectral sensor. The data was collected on a single remote sensing platform for use in coastal mapping and charting activities. Native lidar data is not generally in a format accessible to most Geographical Information Systems (GIS). Specialized in-house and commercial software processes the native lidar data into 3-dimensional positions that can be imported into GIS software for visualization and further analysis.The 3-D position data are used to generate a series of gridded file products, with each covering approximately 5 kilometers of shoreline.
In addition to these lidar point data, the bare earth Digital Elevation Models (DEM) created from the lidar point data are also available. These data are available for custom download at the link provided in the URL section of this metadata record. This data set has 2678.0 columns and 1654.0.The data set was generated from a larger data set and includes all valid data within the requested geographic bounds.

gmd:purpose.gco:CharacterString: These data were collected as a part of the National Coastal Mapping Program to depict the elevations above water in the Florida, Georgia, South Carolina and North Carolina coastal zone.

gmd:credit.gco:CharacterString: Acknowledgement of US Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) Joint Airborne Lidar Bathymetry Technical Center of Expertise (JALBTCX) would be appreciated in products derived from these data.

gmd:fileDescription.gco:CharacterString: This kmz file shows the general extent of the 2010 USACE JALBTCX Topobathy Southeast Lidar data.

gmd:keyword.gco:CharacterString: EARTH SCIENCE > LAND SURFACE > TOPOGRAPHY > TERRAIN ELEVATION
EARTH SCIENCE > LAND SURFACE > TOPOGRAPHY > TERRAIN ELEVATION > DIGITAL ELEVATION/TERRAIN MODEL (DEM)
EARTH SCIENCE > OCEANS > BATHYMETRY/SEAFLOOR TOPOGRAPHY > BATHYMETRY > COASTAL BATHYMETRY
EARTH SCIENCE > OCEANS > COASTAL PROCESSES > COASTAL ELEVATION

CONTINENT > NORTH AMERICA > UNITED STATES OF AMERICA > SOUTH CAROLINA

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Cite As: OCM Partners, [Date of Access]: 2010 USACE NCMP Topobathy Lidar: Atlantic Coast (NC, SC, GA, FL) [Data Date Range], https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/inport/item/50093.

gmd:useLimitation.gco:CharacterString: NOAA provides no warranty, nor accepts any liability occurring from any incomplete, incorrect, or misleading data, or from any incorrect, incomplete, or misleading use of the data. It is the responsibility of the user to determine whether or not the data is suitable for the intended purpose.

gmd:otherConstraints.gco:CharacterString: This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Access Constraints: None

This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Use Constraints: Users should be aware that temporal changes may have occurred since this data set was collected and some parts of this data may no longer represent actual surface conditions. Users should not use this data for critical applications without a full awareness of its limitations.

This data set was compiled dynamically. It will be removed 10 days after 2026-01-30 from the NOAA system. Distribution Liability: Any conclusions drawn from the analysis of this information are not the responsibility of USACE, JALBTCX, the Office for Coastal Management, or its partners.

gmd:title.gco:CharacterString: 2010 USACE NCMP Topobathy Lidar DEM: Atlantic Coast (NC, SC, GA, FL)

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Data Management Plan for this record on InPort.

gmd:environmentDescription.gco:CharacterString: Microsoft Windows XP Version 5.1 (Build 2600) Service Pack 3; ESRI ArcCatalog 9.3.1.4000

gmd:supplementalInformation.gco:CharacterString: A footprint of this data set may be viewed in Google Earth at: https://noaa-nos-coastal-lidar-pds.s3.amazonaws.com/laz/geoid18/1070/supplemental/2010_USACE_JALBTCX_Southeast_Lidar.kmz

gmd:name.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Website

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: NOAA Office for Coastal Management Home Page
Create custom data files by choosing data area, product type, map projection, file format, datum, etc.

gmd:evaluationMethodDescription.gco:CharacterString: Based upon comparison with surveyed ground control points, the vertical accuracy tested better than +/- 0.15 m
The project area was verified for complete coverage.
The data provided in this file were tested against ground truth data. At these locations the lidar data matched within 0.15 meters, and different flight line data agreed within 0.15 meters.

gmd:statement.gco:CharacterString: The USACE collected, processed, and provided the data to the NOAA Office for Coastal Management (OCM). OCM received the data and processed it to be available for custom download from the Data Access Viewer (DAV) and for bulk download from https.

gmd:description.gco:CharacterString: Using MARS software the lidar data were classified according to ASPRS LAS classification standards (1=Unclassified, 2=Ground, 7=Noise, 9=Water) and exported as ASCII files, grouped according to classification (e.g. each file contained only single classification).

The NOAA Office for Coastal Management (OCM) received data in ASCII XYZ format. The files contained LiDAR elevation measurements, however no intensity values were recorded. OCM performed the following processing for data storage and Digital Coast provisioning purposes:
1. The data were converted from ASCII format to las format. 2. All data were converted from orthometric (NAVD88) to ellipsoidal heights using GEOID03. 3. The LAS data were classified according to the ASPRS LAS classification scheme. 4. The LAS header fields were sorted by latitude and updated.
Source Contribution: GPS ground control network | Source Geospatial Form: tabular digital data | Type of Source Media: disc
Source Contribution: LIDAR dataset | Source Geospatial Form: las data | Type of Source Media: disc
The dataset has been mosaiced, reprojected, and clipped as needed from the original input files using PDAL and GDAL commands.
